one_line_risk: R2 isolation keystone is real + non-inert, but the Tracking Center consent KPIs read payload->'properties'->'consent_flags' while the writer persists consent at payload->'consent_flags' — every consent count/flag renders 0/false on a stakeholder-visible, compliance-adjacent deliverable.

tracked_findings.bounce_target: stage-3-build (data/backend on Track C read seam)
tracked_findings.title: consent_flags write/read JSON-path mismatch
tracked_findings.detail: Writer ProcessEventUseCase.ts:177-181 stores consent_flags at payload.consent_flags (top-level of Bronze payload, sibling of properties). Readers get-tracking-health.ts:102,105 and get-recent-events.ts:69 query payload->'properties'->'consent_flags'. SDK capture.ts:85 emits consent_flags top-level only (not inside properties). Net: Tracking Health consent_total/consent_granted and Event Explorer has_consent are ALWAYS 0/false for real events. Fix: correct the two reader JSON paths to payload->'consent_flags' (preferred; matches the envelope's first-class promotion, ADR-1).
tracked_findings.escape_reason: No test drives a consented event into Bronze then reads its consent flag back through the BFF query. tracking-status.test.ts uses hand-built consent fixtures (presentation only); ingest-hardening.e2e asserts the absent-consent QUARANTINE outcome (write-side gate), never a consent-count READ.

tracked_findings.title: dedup-observability E2E asserts metric via incrementCounter directly instead of through CollectorEventConsumer:108-114. Shipped control is correct; only the test assertion is loose. Non-blocking.
audit_log test-read uses superuser pool because audit_log is RLS-disabled by design (forensic SoR). Write path under brain_app. Acceptable, no leak.

near_miss: wired-to-nothing WATCH #2-of-3: migration 0028 resolve_brand_by_install_token was an exact candidate for the built-but-unwired pattern; CONFIRMED WIRED (ProcessEventUseCase:123 -> BronzeRepository:68/83 -> live main.ts:74 default enforceTenantDerivation=true). Not a 3rd occurrence (near-miss correctly closed); no rule-proposal written.
next: stage 3 build (fix FR-CF-01) then re-verify Stage 5 (consent-count read end-to-end) -> back to Stage 6 delta review
